review_title: Worths each penny
description: My experience has been limited to the .NET MAUI library rather than the full Essential Studio suite, but within that scope it has been positive. It saved me significant development time, the controls work reliably, and support resources are generally helpful. I'd recommend it to other MAUI developers looking for a mature component library.
author: Emilio G.
pros: I've used Syncfusion's .NET MAUI controls and they've been a solid addition to my project. The components are well-documented, easy to integrate, and cover a wide range of UI needs that would otherwise take a lot of time to build from scratch. Performance has been good across platforms, and the API feels consistent and predictable. The community license also makes it very accessible for individual developers and small teams to get started.
cons: Some of the controls have a steeper learning curve than expected, and certain advanced customization scenarios required digging deeper into the documentation or sample projects than I would have liked. Occasionally the styling defaults don't match modern design expectations out of the box, so some extra work is needed to get a polished look.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: My impressions
description: Great examples, so useful to get started. But too frequent releases caused issues as behaviour on certain components would unexpectedly change.
author: Paul L.
pros: Value for money, wide offering range.
cons: Nothing

review_title: Excellent toolset to save time i.e money.
description: The documentation is clearly structured and filled with practical examples, making it easier for developers to implement even more advanced components. The components perform reliably and maintain strong performance, including when handling large datasets. Syncfusion also releases regular updates and offers responsive customer support, which helps ensure stability and provides added reassurance when using the platform in long-term or production projects.
author: Michael R.
pros: Syncfusion Essential Studio is a powerful and comprehensive development toolkit that provides an impressive range of UI components and libraries for modern application development. It supports multiple platforms including .NET, JavaScript, Flutter, Angular, React, and Blazor, making it a versatile choice for teams working across different technologies. One of its standout strengths is the breadth and quality of its components—charts, data grids, schedulers, dashboards, and reporting tools are all robust and highly customizable. The documentation is well organized and includes numerous examples, which makes implementation much easier even for complex components. Performance is generally excellent, even with large datasets. Syncfusion also provides frequent updates and strong customer support, which adds confidence when integrating it into long-term projects. Overall, Essential Studio is an excellent all-in-one solution for developers who need reliable, feature-rich UI components without relying on multiple separate libraries.
cons: While Syncfusion Essential Studio is a powerful and comprehensive toolkit, there are a few drawbacks worth noting. The large number of components and frameworks it supports can make the product feel overwhelming at first, especially for new users who only need a small subset of features. Because the suite is so extensive, the learning curve can be steeper than with smaller, specialized libraries. Additionally, the size of the packages can increase project bundle size if developers are not careful about including only the components they need. Some developers also find that customization occasionally requires digging through documentation or support forums. Despite these minor issues, the overall functionality and value of the suite remain strong.

review_title: SyncFusion is a good library overall
description: I appreciate all the effort invested in SyncFusion. My main interest was charting and tables. I have temporarily switched to chart.js, mainly because I have prior experience with it.
author: Peter W.
pros: Value for money, and extensive feature set. There is a good potential to use SyncFusion to solve many problems within a single app.
cons: The interfaces with some of the components are illogical. Also, the documentation is not entirely clear.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Powerful .NET components – exceptional for PDF creation and processing
description: Overall, the experience with Essential Studio has been very positive, especially for .NET development with a strong focus on PDF processing and UI controls. It reliably helps deliver production-grade applications faster while keeping maintenance overhead low.
author: Ralph J.
pros: We have been using Syncfusion across multiple C#/.NET projects, mainly for creating and processing PDF documents in a healthcare software environment. The library is extremely stable, well-documented, and offers an impressive range of features – from precise layout control and form handling to digital signatures. What I value most is the performance: even complex, multi-page PDF outputs with embedded images and charts are generated quickly and reliably. Compared to other libraries, Syncfusion provides outstanding value – an ideal solution for professional .NET developers who depend on stability, strong documentation, and top-tier performance. Pros: Comprehensive set of features for PDF and Office document processing Excellent and responsive technical support High performance and reliability in production environments Great documentation and many code examples Cons: Can take a bit of time to get familiar with the full feature set
cons: Can take a bit of time to get familiar with the full feature set. Github copilot gets sometimes confused and lacks information from the documentation because it uses mostly microsoft learn for up to date infos.

review_title: Syncfusion Essential Studio Review
description: The Support Team does a great job. We've used Syncfusion controls in a number of different applications and tech stacks.
author: Mark A.
pros: Lots of different controls with many options. The PDF libraries have been great. Winform controls have also been veery good with few bugs or hurdles.
cons: Nothing I can think of. I really can't think of anything too negative. I suppose it could always be cheaper.

review_title: Powerful and excellently documented library
author: Jürgen R.
pros: A very powerful and excellently documented library. Highly recommended! We primarily use it for PDF editing and creation.
cons: So far, we haven't had any problems with the library. The excellent documentation makes it easy to find things quickly.

review_title: Feature-Rich Components with Great Documentation
description: Overall, it’s been a solid experience. There’s a bit of a learning curve at the beginning, but once you get used to it, things move pretty fast. I ran into a few issues, but the examples and docs were enough to solve them without contacting support. It definitely saved us a lot of development time.
author: Umut Y.
pros: Covers a lot of needs out of the box (File Manager, editors, Gantt, etc.). Documentation is actually useful, and the AI-assisted part helps a lot when starting. UI looks clean and is flexible enough to fit into different projects. Saves a lot of time compared to building these features from scratch
cons: Some parts feel a bit complex at first, especially Gantt and document editors. Debugging certain issues can take time, as behavior is sometimes not immediately obvious.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Syncfusion Blazor Tools Review
description: Overall my experience with the Rich Text Editor and Syncfusion in general has been very positive. I am looking forward to incorporating more of the components available for Blazor into my project.
author: Marcus W.
pros: So far I have only worked with the Rich Text Editor for Blazor. I found the editor very easy to understand and start working with. The customizability of the tool bar is great and allows you to tailor this editor to your specific needs with ease. I also really appreciated the ability to add a 'SaveUrl', 'RemoveUrl' and 'Path' to the RichTextEditorImageSettings because this made adding images that persist across different users, computers, browsers etc. much easier.
cons: I feel that customizing the appearance of the editor required some digging through documentation which was not as straightforward as some other features.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Great help
description: My experience with Studio has been an immense help in our goal of modernizing all of our software and for future developments
author: Carlos B.
pros: For those of us who design and write software, having tools and components is a great help to maximize development.
cons: to many tools. It is very easy to lose ourselves among so many options, which require some learning.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Value for money products. Great work Syncfusion team, keep it up
description: I’m very satisfied with the Syncfusion products we've used in our projects. Overall, they offer excellent features, comprehensive documentation, and outstanding support and responsiveness to feedback. While there are some minor areas for improvement—particularly in the development and tracking of requested features—my overall experience has been very positive. Our business has also recognized and appreciated the value that Syncfusion products bring to our solutions.
author: Amit S.
pros: They offer a rich variety of controls, features, and well-structured documentation. However, the documentation could be further enhanced by including more customized examples—particularly those based on real-world support requests. This would help developers more easily tailor the controls to their specific needs.
cons: While Essential Studio offers a wide range of powerful controls and strong support, one area that could be improved is the handling of feature requests. In some cases, the development or tracking of requested features can be slower than expected. Enhancing transparency around the roadmap and timelines for user-requested features would make the experience even better.

review_title: Syncfusion Essential Studio, Excellent value for money – a tool I trust
description: Essential Studio is an excellent product — one of only a few of its kind on the global market. I have used other similar products, but the price-to-quality ratio led me to primarily use Syncfusion products today. The technical support is excellent and straightforward, the product quality is high, and it delivers the latest expected functionalities.
author: Igor L.
pros: I mostly use the part related to Blazor. I can say that I’ve been using Syncfusion Blazor components since the very first days of Blazor. Thanks to Syncfusion Blazor controls, my applications look professional and offer an excellent user experience. It’s clear that Syncfusion actively follows rapid changes and continuously adds high-quality new features in line with the expectations of an agile developer.
cons: I have the feeling that sometimes new functionality is released without sufficient testing, which can lead to unexpected or unexplained errors.

review_title: Still excellent after 2 years of full time dev
description: I've been developing a large, complex Blazor-based application for a couple of years. Lots of objects, database back and forth, displaying/editing data in grids, sharing objects with others based on permissions, etc. The Syncfusion controls and Syncfusion support have enabled me to do what would normally take a team of 3 or 4. I tried two other popular control sets in the beginning. I found the first to be lacking in good support and the second to be cost prohibitive. In modern SW dev, where little is protected via proprietary IP, the ones who make the dev process easier & more efficient and set themselves apart with great service will prevail. Syncufusion with their Essential Studio is the epitome of this.
author: Craig M.
pros: The Syncfusion team gets it. Great Controls. Good documentation. Lots of Examples. Continuously adding new controls or updating existing ones. The icing on top: very helpful, polite support with good response times.
cons: If I had to say what needs improvement, I'd suggest two small things: 1) Adding some more detail in the formal documentation. I usually do fine with their documentation and examples, but periodically I'll see the documentation hasn't been updated for the most recent feature set. Or I'm trying to do something pretty unique and have a hard time figuring it out via the examples/documentation. In which case I'll just ask support and they'll respond within a day or two with a nice example. 2) Adding more Property options on the controls for common CSS adjustments. The SF controls already have a lot of good properties to set like Enable/Disable or GridLine Style, etc. But periodically I'll have to add or overwrite CSS to get some behavior. Since blazor is so nicely integrated with C# code, I find it cleaner to use properties.

review_title: Thank you Syncfusion for all you did for us :)
description: We used Syncfusion Essential Studio more than 10 years, until today, we still use them for our document template management and convert to pdf, and RDLC reports to pdf, it is our first solution for converting and management files.
author: Leo X.
pros: fist of all, it is very good third-party tools for us, we used in a lot of our applications, since traditional .net framework project, until Vue Js applications. and we always got very good support from Syncfusion, even for some very old version, still can get help easy and quick. and you can very easy to find what you want from Syncfusion demo sites.
cons: :) so far so good, too many new things to learn and still some bugs need to fix. and some pages in demo sites missing or doesn't show up correctly.

review_title: You should try them for your next project
description: Very productive tools and controls. It gives any application a modern touch. I have been using it for at least 4 years now and they have been solid and great tools and controls in a lot of environments
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: very user friendly. It gives any application a modern touch. I have been using it for at least 4 years now and they have been solid and great tools and controls in a lot of environments
cons: None till now and I have been using it for at least 4 years now and they have been solid and great tools and controls in a lot of environments

review_title: Very good Blazor library that evolves.
description: Very good Blazor library that evolves. New usefuls controls were added. The support is very good. In almost every case there is a solution.
author: Roman S.
pros: It is a feature rich library that contains all the relevant controls like grid, charts, basic controls like ComboBox.
cons: We use the Blazor library but a syntax editor control is missing. A syntax editor exists in other Syncfusion libraries. So the technology is there. The documentation could be better, more complete. For instance: many times it is not clear if the property Width can be set in px or rem.

review_title: Much better than my experience with DevExpress
description: Essential Studio has been a game-changer for our team. The platform is incredibly easy to use, with intuitive tools that streamline development right out of the box. One standout feature is the license management—it’s so smooth it feels basically automatic, removing the usual headaches of setup and compliance. Compared to DevExpress, Essential Studio is far superior in both usability and overall value.
author: Shamar J.
pros: Ease of Use & Quick Adoption High Performance & Flexibility Cross-Platform Coverage (even used with MAUI)
cons: Documentation can be hard to follow in a few use cases Complexity in Some Controls Certain advanced controls (like charts)

review_title: Everything a .NET team needs: quick UI, zero external dependencies, and continuous performance gains
description: Overall, Essential Studio helped me ship .NET apps faster. Performance has been strong in real projects, and recent releases continued to improve load/render times and Excel operations, which kept the UI responsive. And One of the best supports we have received with any Software.
author: Akshit C.
pros: Essential Studio saved us many hours in our .NET front‑end workflow and deployment. The document libraries (PDF, Word/DocIO, Excel/XlsIO, PowerPoint) let me generate, convert, and edit files entirely in C# without Office or Adobe, which kept our export and reporting pipelines simple and reliable. Performance has been excellent: the UI rendered fast and never hampered our app. Upgrades were smooth as well—.NET 9 support was ready when we needed it, so staying current was painless. Integration with our Azure DevOps CI/CD was straightforward since everything ships as NuGet packages that drop cleanly into a .NET build pipeline. And they have the best support. They even go extra mile to help resolve our issues.
cons: I personally haven’t run into any notable issues, but the team broadly notes a learning curve and gaps in advanced customization when you step beyond the demos.

review_title: Reliable and Flexible Rich Text Editing with Syncfusion Essential Studio
description: We currently use Syncfusion specifically for its Rich Text Editor component in our UI applications. The editor provides a robust and user-friendly interface for content creation, with strong support for formatting, links, lists, tables, and media insertion. The component is stable, easy to integrate, and aligns well with our existing UI framework. Its customization options allow us to control the toolbar, validation, and behavior based on business needs. Documentation and examples are clear, which helps with quick implementation and ongoing maintenance. Overall, Syncfusion’s Rich Text Editor has been a reliable and efficient solution for handling rich text input in our applications.
author: Narendra M.
pros: The Rich Text Editor is highly robust and user-friendly, with excellent support for formatting, links, lists, tables, and media. It’s stable, easy to integrate, and fits seamlessly with our existing UI framework. The level of customization for toolbar, validation, and behavior is a big plus.
cons: While the Rich Text Editor itself is very strong, the overall suite can feel a bit heavy if you’re only using one or two components. Also, advanced customizations sometimes require deeper dives into the documentation or samples.

review_title: Good Value with Strong Support, but Requires Effort to Maintain
description: Overall, Essential Studio provides strong value for the cost, with feature-rich components and a support team that actively works with customers to resolve issues. While the editors aren’t perfect and there are trade-offs in styling and maintainability, the product has been reliable enough for production use, and the pace of updates ensures long-term viability. For teams prepared to manage frequent updates and occasionally provide detailed bug reports, Essential Studio can be a cost-effective and powerful option.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The document editor components are strong and integrate well into a larger system. Syncfusion’s customer support is very responsive and generally quick to provide fixes or workarounds, especially when issues are reproducible. Their frequent release cycle means improvements and bug fixes get delivered quickly. Considering the overall licensing cost, the value for money is solid compared to competitors.
cons: The online document editors still have a fair number of bugs, and complex use cases can expose edge cases easily. Syncfusion often requires a working sample project to reproduce an issue before offering fixes, which is difficult when working with complex, enterprise-level applications where extracting a minimal example isn’t realistic. The rapid update cycle, while helpful, can also be a burden to keep up with. Styling consistency across controls is not as seamless as with some competing libraries, so we often use other UI libraries for cleaner out-of-the-box design.

review_title: Comprehensive and stable UI toolkit for professional applications
description: Essential Studio is a stable and comprehensive UI toolkit, suitable for professional applications. Integration into projects is straightforward, the documentation is clear, and performance remains reliable even under heavy load. Customer support is responsive and helpful. We use Essential Studio primarily for processing and generating PDF files in connection with electronic invoicing (ZUGFeRD / FacturX). It has significantly reduced our workload while making our main product, ISEntial – an ERP system with integrated financial accounting – even more robust and efficient. I highly recommend Essential Studio to any development team looking for a reliable, well-documented, and performance-oriented UI toolkit.
author: René K.
pros: - Extensive UI components - Easy to integrate into projects - Good documentation - Stable performance under high load - Support for multiple operating systems - Responsive and helpful customer support
cons: We have not encountered any drawbacks while using Essential Studio, and it has fully met our expectations.

review_title: Development efficiency has significantly improved, the components are stable, and the technical support is excellent.
description: My overall experience with Essential Studio has been very positive. The interface is user-friendly, the components are abundant and easy to integrate, which greatly enhances development efficiency. The documentation is detailed, and solutions can be found quickly whenever problems arise. In addition, technical support responds promptly and helps resolve technical issues. Throughout the usage, each component has been highly stable, allowing me to focus on developing application features with confidence.
author: 常瑋 羅.
pros: The out-of-the-box UI suite of Essential Studio allows us to quickly focus on developing business logic and enables us to build robust and highly interactive applications in no time.
cons: The initial learning curve is relatively steep, requiring more time to read the documentation, but this is also because the component is well-featured.

review_title: Top Notch Products Backed By A Qualified and Dedicated Support Staff
description: Overall, I am very happy with my experience with the Essential Studio products that I have worked with.
author: Rick B.
pros: The Syncfusion products that I have used are very helpful and make me a more productive and efficient developer. Their products often come with capabilities that a programmer longs for; capabilities that can help us to 'hit the ground running' and not get bogged down in repeatedly programming what should be immediately available to us. Just as impressive, Syncfusion's one-on-one Support communication continues to be extremely reliable. Post any question about any product and within 24 hours (during a work week) you will get a response that you know strives to directly answer your specific question. I have never received a completely unhelpful or overly generalized response. Understandably, sometimes follow-up clarification is needed. However, one always feels that the Syncfusion Support team is trying their best to react to your exact needs. Their follow-up responses always reflect that. In fact, I have the impression that Syncfusion Support will not be fully satisfied until I am fully satisfied. Their answers are articulate and often come with impressive hand-crafted and very specific demonstrations of the solutions they are trying to convey to you.
cons: As I've stated, I am extremely happy with Syncfusion's one-on-one support. That said, with respect, their on-line page documentation could use a bit of attention. Those pages sometimes lack an intuitive navigational flow that might help a programmer walk the path to a full answer with more ease. To be clear, for the most part, answers can indeed be found within the various pages. I just wish the search was a bit easier. I know Syncfusion is aware of this issue and I am grateful that they express a continuing dedication to addressing it.

review_title: Fast and realible development
author: Enrique P.
pros: What impresses me the most is the wide variety of Blazor components available. Whether you're building complex data grids, rich dashboards, or interactive forms, Syncfusion provides an incredibly comprehensive set of tools that makes development much faster and more efficient. What truly stands out is the performance of these Blazor components. They are fast, responsive, and integrate seamlessly into any Blazor project. The components feel lightweight despite their rich functionality, which significantly improves the user experience in web applications.
cons: One area where Syncfusion could improve is bug resolution. While the overall quality of the components is high, there are times when certain issues—especially with specific Blazor components—take a bit too long to get fixed. It's not a deal-breaker, but quicker turnaround on bug fixes would definitely enhance the overall developer experience.

review_title: Powerful Gantt component for complex planning (review limited to the Gantt module)
description: Overall, our experience with the Essential Studio Gantt has been positive. It is a powerful, mature component that can support complex planning requirements in a SaaS product, provided you’re prepared for a learning curve and some custom logic. It’s a strong choice for teams building advanced project planning tools or PMO-oriented products, but may feel heavy for simpler or lightweight Gantt needs. The value really shows when you fully leverage its depth and flexibility.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The Gantt module is extremely feature-rich and well suited for complex project planning use cases. It handles dependencies, sub-tasks, drag-and-drop, resizing, and hierarchical data very reliably. Once configured, interactions are smooth and the UI feels mature and professional. It’s particularly strong for structured, enterprise-grade planning scenarios where waterfall or dependency-driven logic is required. The support team was also responsive and helpful when dealing with advanced configuration questions related to the Gantt.
cons: The Gantt’s default Auto-Scheduling logic is very strict and waterfall-oriented, which can be limiting if you want more Agile or Jira-like behavior (for example, manually controlled parent task dates). Supporting that type of logic requires additional configuration and custom handling. From a technical standpoint: Strict NPM versioning can create friction in CI/CD pipelines, including license key issues across environments Preventing UI flickering on frequent updates may require manual diffing logic Documentation covers standard scenarios well, but edge cases and deep customizations take time to figure out Adding highly customized UI elements on top of the Gantt can sometimes be constrained
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months
